title: tussilago gmelinii
intro: Tussilago gmelinii, also known as Farfara or Russian coltsfoot, is a perennial plant native to Eastern Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow dandelion-like flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. Tussilago gmelinii has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and even exhibiting potential antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often infused in hot water to make a tea, or dried and used as a powder in capsules or tinctures.
